name: Alhambra

notes: Every frozen row was reconciled against Alhambra's captured restaurant-issued menu page on 2026-07-14.

The current first-party page publishes 140 raw structured presentations across Breakfast, Brunch, Lunch, Dinner, and Veranda. Excluding four alcohol-only rows and six CMS/package fragments leaves 130 food/nonalcoholic presentations and 107 current formulations.
The correction consolidates 23 duplicate meal-period, punctuation, or spelling presentations while retaining each exact published presentation as evidence.
The frozen catalog included five nonproduct/alcohol artifacts, represented two formulations twice under spelling or meal-period variants, and omitted the current Selection of Juice formulation.
Restaurant-published typos are retained as aliases and source presentation names while the canonical app names normalize obvious errors such as Avacado, Butermilk, and Mediterr Aenean.
Alhambra publishes current item names and selected ingredient descriptions, but no complete recipe-level allergen matrix or cross-contact disclosure.
Seventy-five current formulations have positive contains signals from explicit fixed components or unavoidable named formats; 32 remain unavailable for fixed allergen data.

Of 113 frozen rows, 46 allergen results were wrong: 35 underreported fixed signals, nine overreported optional or unsupported signals, and two both omitted fixed signals and promoted optional choices.

Optional add-ons and selectable proteins or entrees are not promoted into fixed claims. This removes fixed fish from Avocado Toast, fish and sesame from Alhambra Platter, and fish/shellfish from Organic Quinoa Salad while retaining its fixed burrata and pistachio signals.
Absent menu text is represented as unavailable, not allergen-free. No global may-contain, shared-equipment, or negative safety claim was found.

The current page exposes a stable application/ld+json Menu payload, but the frozen generic website extraction flattened meal-period presentations into standalone rows without package, optional-choice, alcohol, or duplicate semantics.
That context loss created standalone Astor component headings, retained two isolated alcohol cocktails, failed to consolidate spelling and meal-period aliases, and omitted Selection of Juice.

The correction parses the five source menus, preserves their menu/section presentation context, excludes only enumerated CMS and alcohol rows, consolidates verified formulation aliases, and applies fixed-allergen semantics after optional-choice text is separated.
A guarded repair requires exact current formulation, presentation, category, source-type, and allergen invariants before replacing the frozen snapshot.

sourceAttempts.outcome: Captured HTTP 200 restaurant-issued HTML and exhaustively reviewed its five structured menus, 27 source sections, and 140 raw menu-item presentations.
notes: The retained artifact is 137,768 bytes with SHA-256 5eca44a82a4617915dce48574a5f4c9ad1b69088a822a582ab42a0fca03ce6fc.
The page footer identifies Alhambra at 923 16th Street NW, Washington, DC 20006 and publishes 2026 hours.

sourceAttempts.outcome: Found current fixed menu descriptions but no complete allergen matrix, recipe disclosure, cross-contact policy, shared-equipment statement, or allergen-specific legend.
notes: Positive claims therefore stop at directly published fixed components and unavoidable named formats.

findings.summary: The frozen 113-row extraction failed to preserve the current structured menu's package, alcohol, alias, and presentation semantics.
findings.resolution: Rebuilt 107 current formulations from 130 accepted source presentations, removed five frozen artifacts, consolidated duplicate spellings and meal-period presentations, and restored Selection of Juice.

findings.summary: Forty-six frozen rows had incorrect allergen results because the extraction both missed published fixed components and promoted optional choices or unsupported culinary assumptions.
findings.resolution: Corrected 35 underreports, nine overreports, and two mixed errors; mapped 75 fixed-positive formulations; kept 32 unsupported formulations unavailable; and did not invent cross-contact or negative claims.

findings.summary: The generic parser flattened a rich five-menu JSON-LD payload, turning package components and isolated alcohol entries into products and losing formulation identity across meal periods.
findings.resolution: Prepared a source-specific, captured-payload catalog with exact presentation provenance, enumerated exclusions, alias consolidation, optional-choice separation, and guarded output invariants.

repairs.summary: Prepared and focused-tested a 107-formulation, 130-presentation Alhambra catalog with 75 fixed-positive rows, 32 unavailable rows, and all 113 frozen rows terminally reconciled.
verificationCommands: RESTAURANT_REPOSITORY_PATH=tmp/restaurant-verification/alhambra-isolated.generated.json node scripts/repair-final-generated-regressions.mjs (isolated copy; verified 107 items, 24 canonical categories, 75 fixed-positive rows, 32 unavailable rows, exclusion of CMS/alcohol artifacts, optional-choice semantics, zero variant groups, and idempotent Alhambra guard behavior)

notes: Corrected snapshot SHA-256: 5d68c10a95aebc3021e8b940f9d422316771a68ad8856d29228678b775173cec.

Reconciliation counts: {"dispositions":{"exact_match":101,"variant_match":7,"artifact":5},"allergens":{"verified":34,"mismatch":46,"accurately_unavailable":28,"not_applicable":5},"mismatchKinds":{"underreported":35,"overreported":9,"mixed":2}}.
Current corrected catalog counts: 107 formulations, 130 presentations, 24 canonical categories, 75 fixed-positive rows, and 32 unavailable rows.
